sections.paragraph:
ἀδηλότης, ητος, ἡ uncertainty (Protagoras [V b.c.] et al.; Polyb., Plut., Philo al.) ἐλπίζειν ἐπὶ πλούτου ἀδηλότητι to rest one’s hope in such an uncertain thing as wealth 1 Ti 6:17 (Dio Chrys. 16, 8; B-D-F §165). S. δῆλος.

Protagoras Protagoras , V–VI a.d.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

Polyb Polyb , III–II b.c.—List 5

Plut Plut , I–II a.d.—List 5

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

  1. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)

Dio Dio Chrys , I–II a.d.—List 5

B-D-F B-D-F = FBlass, ADebrunner, RFunk, A Greek Grammar of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 19
